insight: Japan's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with no significant change in their pace of play. This suggests that the breaks did not disrupt their rhythm, and they were able to maintain their intensity after the brief pauses. When Japan was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to keep their momentum going afterwards, which is a positive sign, although the sample size is limited to just one instance. Overall, the statistics indicate that Japan has been able to use hydration breaks to their advantage, maintaining their pace and momentum, which could be a valuable asset in high-stakes tournament matches.
